DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JEREMIAH JOSEPH A/K/A JERRY MOTAK'S MOTION TO QUASH DEFENDANTS' DEPOSITION SUBPOENA TO MARSHALL MEDICAL CENTER - MOTION GRANTED IN PART; DENIED IN PART. THE COURT AGREES THAT DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO THE MEDICAL RECORDS AT ISSUE REGARDING ANY "INTERNAL BLEEDING, ULCERS OR STOMACH COMPLAINTS, TESTS, TREATMENT OR MEDICAL OPINIONS" FROM 9/25/01 TO PRESENT. HOWEVER, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE REQUEST FOR RECORDS RELATING TO "ANY PHYSICAL DISABILITY" TO BE VASTLY OVERBROAD. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion T Compel Defendants Attendance At Deposition In Compliance With The Notice Of Deposition With Production Of Documents And Compel Defendants' Further Responses To Plaintff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. 1 And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jennifer Zarich, Esq. (415-978-9999 ext 11) for Jeremiah Motak, Plainitff and moving party.
Dwight Bishop, Esq. (925-939-2544) for Daksha Patel, Defendant and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
PLAINTIFF MOTAK, JEREMIAH JOSEPH A/K/A JERRY MOTAK's Motion To Compel Defendants Attendance At Deposition In Compliance With The Notice Of Deposition With Production Of Documents And Compel Defendants' Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. 1 And For Monetary Sanctions is Granted. No timely opposition filed. Sanctions is granted in the amount of $2495.00. Order is signed in open Court. See scanned order for details. Judge Pro Tem: James Watson, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-04-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-09-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Defts' Deposition Subpoena To Marshall Medical Center
Off Calendar Mar-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNIFER ZARICH, TEL. NO. 978-9999 EXT. 11 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF JEREMIAH JOSEPH A/K/A JERRY MOTAK'S MOTION To Quash Defendants' Deposition Subpoena To Marshall Medical Center -
Motion granted in part; denied in part. The Court agrees that Defendant is entitled to the medical records at issue regarding any "internal bleeding, ulcers or stomach complaints, tests, treatment or medical opinions" from 9/25/01 to present. However, the Court finds that the request for records relating to "any physical disability" to be vastly overbroad. All requests for sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/JPT
